highlights.description: Pure Go library for reading and writing Microsoft Outlook PST files
Full support for both ANSI and Unicode PST formats
Transaction-safe write operations with two-phase commit

added.description: Primitive types for PST parsing: NodeID, BlockID, HeapID, PropID, GUID
Disk layer: header parsing, page/block structures, CRC computation, encryption/decryption
NDB layer: node database with NBT/BBT B-tree search, node data reading, subnode support
LTP layer: Heap-on-Node, BTree-on-Heap, Property Context, Table Context, named properties
RTF decompression using LZFu algorithm for compressed message bodies
Messaging layer: PST, Folder, Message, Attachment, Recipient types with lazy loading
Search folder support for virtual folders with search criteria
Disk layer write support: allocation maps, header/page/block serialization
NDB layer write support: B-tree modifications, node creation/deletion
Transaction system with two-phase commit and crash recovery
LTP layer write support: heap allocation, BTH modifications, property bag updates
Messaging layer write support: PST creation, folder operations, MessageBuilder API
`pstinfo` CLI tool for inspecting PST file contents
Go 1.23+ iterator support for idiomatic folder and message iteration

documentation.description: Comprehensive documentation: getting started, user guides, architecture, API reference
MS-PST specification mapping with inline code references
Case study documenting implementation lessons and debugging techniques
README with features, quick start, and API examples

tests.description: Unit tests for disk layer CRC computation and encryption
Unit tests for LTP property types and GUID parsing
Sample PST file for integration testing

build.description: Go module definition requiring Go 1.23+ for iter.Seq2 support
